Options: "Approved — proceed", "I want to review the Realignment Feature design", "Skip Realignment Feature — I'll handle the gap manually."

sub_step.title: Architect Critic — Review All Changes
sub_step.action: Set realignment_step=6 in state.json (crash guard).

Spawn Architect in Critic role via new_task using the realignment_critic_prompt from common patterns. Critic reads ALL vision files, ALL planning files, and updated roadmap. Checks for: contradictions between vision and planning, missing abstraction coverage, roadmap dependency violations, Realignment Feature completeness, planning file internal consistency. Do NOT propose new features or expand scope.
Wait for Critic to complete.

If Critic finds issues tracing back to vision: increment realignment_loop_count in state.json. If realignment_loop_count < 2 (first or second loop): loop to Step B3. If realignment_loop_count >= 2 (third B3 pass): escalate to user — "The Critic keeps finding vision-level issues after [X] rounds. The vision change may be fundamentally incompatible." Options: "Restart from Phase 1 (re-vision from scratch)", "Accept Critic's findings as documented risks and proceed to B7", "I'll manually resolve the contradictions." Do NOT loop again.
If Critic finds issues in planning only: re-spawn Architect (Step B5) with targeted fixes.

sub_step.title: PD Final Review — Comprehensive Alignment Check
sub_step.action: Set realignment_step=7 in state.json (crash guard).
Determine what was modified: use git diff against pre-pipeline state, or track files from completion summaries in Steps B3-B6.

Read and verify: every changed vision file, every changed planning file, the updated roadmap (ALL features — verify forward dependencies hold), current-state.md (update with vision realignment summary), state.json (verify total_features matches roadmap).

Checklist: roadmap forward-dependencies hold, total_features matches, no orphaned references, Realignment Feature properly numbered/positioned, no vision-planning contradictions, E2E impact documented.
Set realignment_step=null and realignment_loop_count=0 in state.json.
Proceed to Step 1 — the next feature (which may be the Realignment Feature).

step.title: Read State and Identify Next Feature
step.action: Read state.json. If current_feature_index == total_features: set status="complete" and total_replans=0 in state.json, announce project completion, and STOP. Do NOT read roadmap.md. Do NOT proceed to any further steps — the project is finished.

If current_feature_index < total_features: read roadmap.md to locate the feature at position current_feature_index + 1. If no feature exists at that position (roadmap may have been edited), flag to user.

step.title: Construct the Feature-Developer Prompt
step.action: Build a comprehensive prompt using the feature_developer_prompt_construction pattern from common patterns. Include:

required_elements.element: Feature position: "You are building Feature [X] of [Y] — [Name] from [Wave]."
The feature line from the wave map (name, 1-line description, dependencies). The FD Architect uses this as the high-level scope.
Paths to ALL files in the planning/ directory — list the directory and include every .md file found. The FD Architect uses these to understand the architecture and abstractions.

Pre-resolved vision file paths: before constructing this prompt, read vision/vision-config.json to determine project_type. Then read vision-summary.md's Feature Index table (Feature | Tier | Artifacts Used | Data Entities). Locate the target feature and extract the specific artifacts and data entities listed for it.
Project-type-adaptive artifact paths (use the format matching vision/vision-config.json project_type): - GUI: "screens/[name].md and screens/[name].html" - CLI: "commands/[name].md and commands/[name].session.md" - API: "endpoints/[name].md and endpoints/[name].openapi.yaml" - Library: "public-api/[name].md and public-api/[name].example.*" - Game: "screens/[name].md + screens/[name].html AND mechanics/[name].md" - Pipeline: "pipeline-stages/[name].md with Mermaid diagrams" If vision/vision-config.json does not exist: default to GUI (screens/) — backward compatible.
Include the EXACT file paths for the project type — features/[feature-name].md, [artifact-type]/[artifact-a].md, [artifact-type]/[artifact-b].md (canonical AI-mode specs), data/[entity-x].md, data/[entity-y].md — plus vision-summary.md and feature-inventory.md for index context. Also note any companion deliverable files (HTML mocks, OpenAPI YAML, session transcripts, usage examples, Mermaid diagrams) for reference. Do NOT dump entire directory paths — provide only the files relevant to this feature as determined by the Feature Index.

Instruction: "Read the vision docs for this feature. Derive acceptance criteria from the vision. Create an implementation plan using the architecture and abstractions from planning/." The FD Architect owns acceptance criteria — the wave map provides only the feature name, 1-line description, and dependencies. The specific vision files for this feature are listed above — read those exact files to derive acceptance criteria.
Completion instruction from common patterns feature_developer_prompt_construction.
Context about previously built features from current-state.md "What the App Can Currently Do". If more than 3 prior features, summarize concisely (≤2 sentences).

Feature Roadmap Summary: Read roadmap.md and compile a concise list of ALL features — one line per feature (name + wave). Format: - Wave 1 (Foundation): - [x] Feature 1: [Name] — [1-line description] - [x] Feature 2: [Name] — [1-line description] - Wave 2 (Skeleton): - [x] Feature 3: [Name] — [1-line description] - Wave 3 (Vertical Slices): - [ ] Feature 4: [Name] — [1-line description] - → Feature 5: [Name] — [1-line description] ← YOU ARE HERE - [ ] Feature 6: [Name] — [1-line description] - Wave 4 (Cross-Cutting): - [ ] Feature 7: [Name] — [1-line description] Mark completed features with [x], pending with [ ], and the current feature with → ← YOU ARE HERE. Use wave grouping. This gives FD the big picture: what was built before, what's being built now, and what's coming next. FD uses this when reporting scope drift (can identify if drift touches areas future features will cover) or when the user asks for something that aligns with a future feature. If there are more than 15 total features, collapse completed waves to a one-line summary (e.g., "Wave 1 (Foundation): Features 1-4 complete ✓") and only expand the current wave.

Hands-off mode: read hands_off_mode from state.json. Pass as literal boolean in the FD spawn prompt: "hands_off_mode: true" or "hands_off_mode: false". FD uses this to skip user interaction gates and defer GUI smoke tests to feature end.

Smoke test automation configuration: read the full smoke_test_automation block from state.json. Pass the entire block (enabled, environments with all fields including mcp_available/mcp_name/verified, missing_prerequisites) to FD as literal config in the spawn prompt. FD stores this in its state.json and passes it to Code mode subtasks. If smoke_test_automation.enabled is false or not yet configured, pass enabled: false.

step.title: Spawn Feature-Developer Subtask
step.action: BEFORE spawning: set fd_status="spawned" in state.json.
Spawn feature-developer via new_task with the full prompt from Step 2.
If new_task fails: immediately set fd_status="idle" in state.json. Report error to user.
Wait for the feature-developer to return with attempt_completion.
When FD returns: set fd_status="completed" in state.json.

step.title: Process Feature-Developer Completion
step.action: Read the FD's completion summary. Note: branch name, feature name, architectural patterns reported.
Set current_branch_name in state.json to the branch_name from FD's completion summary.

branch.condition: FD returned with error or incomplete feature
branch.action: Before routing to error handling: check the FD's completion summary for the phrase "Feature abandoned due to major scope drift — user requested restart." If present: the user intentionally abandoned this feature from within the FD context. Do NOT re-ask the user. Set fd_status="idle" in state.json. Document in current-state.md: "Feature [N] abandoned — user requested restart due to scope drift." Offer the user: "Re-spawn Architect to revise the roadmap removing this feature", "Re-spawn FD for this feature with adjusted scope", "Skip to next feature." Proceed based on user choice. Do NOT route to feature_developer_subtask_fails.
If the completion summary does NOT indicate intentional abandonment: route to error_handling feature_developer_subtask_fails scenario. Do NOT proceed to Phase 4. Do NOT set fd_status="completed".

branch.condition: FD completion summary is malformed (missing required fields)
branch.action: Route to error_handling fd_completion_malformed scenario. Do NOT proceed to Phase 4.

Verify FD archived its artifacts: check that agent-workspace/feature/archive/ contains plan-[sanitized].md, ref-[sanitized].md, and state-[sanitized].json. If archive files are missing: route to error_handling fd_completion_malformed scenario with note "Archive files missing despite valid completion summary."
Set phase_4_substep="pending_verification" in state.json (crash guard: if context is lost during verification, resume knows to re-verify).

Independent roadmap verification: do NOT trust the FD's self-reported scope-drift assessment alone. Cross-reference four sources: (a) the roadmap entry, (b) the FD's completion summary and archived plan, (c) the git diff of the feature branch, and (d) the actual project files. Perform these checks: (0) Git-diff level: Read current_branch_name from state.json. If not null, determine the trunk branch (main, master, or develop — whichever exists locally). Before running the diff, read the roadmap's wave descriptions to build a mental inventory: which directories and files were established in Wave 1 (Foundation — core abstractions, interfaces, data model, project scaffold) and Wave 2 (Skeleton — app shell, routing, all screen shells)? These are protected files — Wave 3 features should extend them, not modify them. Then run `git diff --stat trunk...feature-branch`. This lists every file changed across the entire feature. Cross-reference the diff against: (i) the roadmap entry's expected scope, (ii) your Wave 1-2 inventory — any modification to a Wave 1-2 file is a red flag regardless of change size, (iii) unexpected domains or directories not mentioned in the roadmap. If the FD reported no drift but the diff shows unexpected files or Wave 1-2 modifications: flag this as hidden feature-level drift. If git_automation_enabled is false or current_branch_name is null: skip the diff and rely on spot-checks alone. (1) Document-level: Read the FD's archived plan at agent-workspace/feature/archive/plan-[sanitized].md to extract the acceptance criteria the FD Architect derived from vision docs. Does the FD's completion summary address every acceptance criterion from its own plan? Does the FD's scope-drift assessment acknowledge the same scope changes visible in the diff and archived plan? (2) Project-level: Use the diff file list as your systematic map (instead of random spot-checks). Read key files from each modified directory to verify expected patterns exist. If the diff shows unexpected directories or modified Wave 1-2 files, investigate those specifically — these are the highest-risk drift signals. (3) Knowledge-level: Are the knowledge file updates consistent with the roadmap entry's expected patterns AND the actual diff? If discrepancies are found, flag to user: "The feature differs from the roadmap entry. FD reported [assessment], but I observe [discrepancies] in [diff / project files / knowledge files / completion summary]. Vision and roadmap will be re-evaluated in Phase 4 Step 5. How proceed now?" Options: "Proceed to Phase 4 — drift will be fully assessed during vision re-evaluation", "I will manually update vision/roadmap files now before Phase 4", "I'll review the drift myself and come back."
Set phase_4_substep=null AND current_phase=4 in state.json (override the "pending_verification" sentinel — verification is complete; Phase 4 begins).
Proceed to Phase 4.

phase.name: phase_4_qa_and_architectural_audit
phase.title: QA & Architectural Alignment Audit + Vision Re-evaluation
phase.trigger: When the feature-developer subtask returns successfully from Phase 3
phase.overview: Eight gates after every feature: (1) Run unit/integration tests — catch code regressions. (2) Verify FD's E2E regression report — read-only confirmation that FD ran and passed the existing E2E suite. No re-run. (3) Evaluate scope drift — cross-reference FD completion, git diff, roadmap, vision. (4) Generate new E2E test from roadmap E2E spec — spawn Code subtask. (5) Validate new E2E test — read file, fix if broken. (6) Architectural audit. (7) Vision re-evaluation. (8) Dashboard update.

step.title: Unit & Integration Regression Testing
step.action: Run the project's unit and integration test suites. Discover the test commands by inspecting the project's build configuration — this varies by tech stack (see planning/tech-stack.md for the project's stack). Common patterns: Node.js → package.json scripts (`npm test`, `npm run test:unit`, `npm run test:integration`); .NET → `dotnet test` (runs all test projects in the solution); Python → `pytest`, `python -m pytest tests/`; Java → `./gradlew test` or `mvn test`; Go → `go test ./...`; Rust → `cargo test`. If a single command runs both unit and integration tests together (e.g., `dotnet test`, `cargo test`, `go test ./...`), run it once — it covers both. If the project has separate commands for unit and integration tests, run both. If no test command can be discovered, ask the user for the command. If the user confirms no test suite exists, note "⚠️ No test suite configured" in the audit report and proceed to Step 2.

branch.action: Set phase_4_substep="unit_tests_passed" in state.json. Proceed to Step 2.
Spawn a code subtask to fix regressions using test_fix_prompt_pattern from common patterns. Include the failing test output.
Wait for completion, then re-run tests.
Set phase_4_substep="unit_tests_passed". Proceed to Step 2.
Present to user via ask_followup_question: "Retry fix", "Skip tests (not recommended)", "I'll fix manually". Wait for response.
If user resolves the issue (manually or via retry) and tests pass: set phase_4_substep="unit_tests_passed" in state.json. Proceed to Step 2.

If user skips tests: set phase_4_substep="unit_tests_passed" (acknowledging skip). Document in current-state.md as "⚠️ Unit tests skipped for this feature — manual verification performed." Proceed to Step 2.

step.title: Verify FD's E2E Regression Report
step.description: The feature-developer runs the full existing E2E suite as its own regression gate before completing. FD guarantees E2E passes — it does not complete until E2E is green. This step reads FD's completion summary to confirm E2E regression was performed and passed. No E2E re-run — FD's guarantee is trusted.
step.action: Read the FD's archived state.json at agent-workspace/feature/archive/state-[sanitized].json. Check the e2e_regression_status field. Possible values: "passed" (all existing E2E tests passed), "skipped" (no existing E2E tests), null (E2E regression not recorded — FD may predate this field). This field persists across session boundaries — no reliance on ephemeral completion summary text.

branch.action: Announce: "✅ E2E Regression: FD reports all existing tests pass. Verified (no re-run)."
Set phase_4_substep="e2e_regression_passed" in state.json. Proceed to Step 3.
Announce: "No existing E2E tests — regression gate skipped (nothing to regress)."
Set phase_4_substep="e2e_regression_passed" in state.json. Proceed to Step 3.

branch.condition: FD's state.json has e2e_regression_status=null (E2E regression not recorded)
branch.action: FD's state.json has no e2e_regression_status — the E2E regression gate was either not run or the FD's workflow predates the e2e_regression_status field. Do NOT run E2E yourself. Flag to the user via ask_followup_question: "FD's completion summary does not include E2E regression results. The FD may not have run the E2E regression gate (older workflow version). How proceed?" Options: 1. "Proceed — trust the feature is correct (E2E regression will catch issues on next feature)" 2. "I'll run the E2E suite manually and report back" 3. "Re-spawn FD with updated workflow to re-run E2E regression"

If user selects Proceed: document in current-state.md as "⚠️ E2E regression not confirmed for this feature — FD report missing." Set phase_4_substep="e2e_regression_passed" (acknowledging gap). Proceed to Step 3.
If user selects Manual run: wait for user to report results. If passing: proceed to Step 3. If failing: escalate — this is a regression FD should have caught.
If user selects Re-spawn FD: the FD will resume in Phase 5 and run the E2E regression gate. Wait for FD to return. Then re-read the completion summary. Loop back to this verification.

step.title: Evaluate Scope Drift
step.description: Cross-reference the FD's completion against the roadmap, vision, git diff, and actual project files. Catching drift here — before E2E generation and audit — prevents building E2E tests for misaligned code.
step.action: Read the feature's entry from roadmap.md: feature name, 1-line description, dependencies, and E2E spec.
Read the FD's completion summary for the scope-drift self-assessment.

Cross-reference four sources: (a) the roadmap entry for this feature, (b) the FD's completion summary and archived plan (agent-workspace/feature/archive/plan-[sanitized].md), (c) the git diff of the feature branch (if available — same diff from Phase 3 Step 4), and (d) the actual project files. If git diff is unavailable: use knowledge/architecture.md for structural file inventory and the archived plan for the per-step file list.

When the git diff IS available: use its file list as a systematic map. Pay special attention to: (i) Wave 1-2 abstraction files appearing in the diff — these should almost never be modified by Wave 3+ features, (ii) directories that don't match the feature's domain, (iii) new shared utilities or config changes not mentioned in the roadmap.

branch.condition: NO DRIFT — built feature matches roadmap entry and vision
branch.action: Set phase_4_substep="scope_evaluated" in state.json. Proceed to Step 4.

branch.condition: MINOR DRIFT — small discrepancies that don't affect other features
branch.action: PD edits the relevant vision .md file(s) and ALL planning/ .md file(s) directly to reflect reality. List the planning/ directory and edit every .md file that needs updating. If any new screens, data entities, or feature-artifact mappings were added, update vision-summary.md's Feature Index table. If the drift involved a technology change, also update the relevant component entry in tech-sourcing.md. If new E2E patterns emerged (selector conventions, helper methods, infrastructure quirks), update e2e-authoring-guide.md. Note in current-state.md under "Vision Updates": what changed and why.
If the drift changes what should be tested end-to-end, update the feature's roadmap E2E spec directly in roadmap.md.
Set phase_4_substep="scope_evaluated" in state.json. Proceed to Step 4.

branch.condition: MAJOR DRIFT — changes that affect remaining roadmap features OR unexpected modification of Wave 1-2 infrastructure
branch.action: Flag for user via ask_followup_question: "Major scope drift detected. [Describe the specific divergence and which remaining features it affects.] How proceed?" Options: 1. "Re-plan remaining features with updated vision" 2. "Accept the divergence — update vision docs but don't re-plan" 3. "I'll manually update the vision and roadmap"
Set phase_4_substep="replan_in_progress" in state.json.

PD edits the relevant vision .md file(s) and ALL planning/ .md file(s) directly to reflect the detected drift. List the planning/ directory and edit every .md file that needs updating. The vision docs must be current BEFORE spawning the Architect re-plan.
Spawn Architect with: updated vision docs, list of completed features (1 through current_feature_index), and instruction to revise roadmap for remaining features only — including their E2E specs.

After Architect returns: validate total_features >= current_feature_index. Present revised roadmap for user approval (same gate as Phase 2 Step 4). Update total_features and increment total_replans after approval.
After re-plan approved: set phase_4_substep="scope_evaluated". Proceed to Step 4.

PD edits vision docs and ALL planning/ .md files to match reality. List the planning/ directory and edit every .md file that needs updating. If any new screens, data entities, or feature-artifact mappings were added, update vision-summary.md's Feature Index table. If the divergence involved a technology change, also update the relevant component entry in tech-sourcing.md. Update roadmap E2E spec if needed. Set phase_4_substep="scope_evaluated". Proceed to Step 4.
Wait for user. When ready, re-read vision docs and roadmap. Set phase_4_substep="scope_evaluated". Proceed to Step 4.

step.title: Generate New E2E Test from Roadmap E2E Spec
step.description: Read the feature's E2E spec from roadmap.md (written by Architect during wave map). Spawn a Code subtask to generate the E2E test.

sub_step.title: Read Roadmap E2E Spec
sub_step.action: Read the feature's entry from roadmap.md. Extract the E2E spec line.

branch.action: Announce: "E2E not applicable for this feature — [reason from roadmap]."

Set phase_4_substep="e2e_validated" in state.json. Skip to Step 6 (Audit) — no E2E test to generate or validate. (Setting e2e_validated (not e2e_generated) ensures resume routing goes directly to Step 6, not Step 5 which would try to read a non-existent file.)

sub_step.action: Sanitize feature name for file: lowercase, spaces/special-chars → hyphens.

sub_step.title: Idempotency Check
sub_step.action: Ensure tests/e2e/ directory exists. Check if tests/e2e/feature-[sanitized-name].* already exists.

branch.action: Announce: "E2E test file already exists: [filename]. Skipping generation (resume)."
Set phase_4_substep="e2e_generated" in state.json. Proceed to Step 5 (validate existing file).

sub_step.title: Spawn the E2E Generation Subtask
sub_step.action: Spawn Code mode subtask via new_task using the e2e_test_generation_prompt from common patterns.

Include: tech stack context from planning/, the E2E authoring guide at planning/e2e-authoring-guide.md (MANDATORY — the subtask MUST read this file before writing any test code; it defines the project's E2E conventions, framework, selector strategies, helper patterns, and checklist), feature name (sanitized), and the EXACT E2E spec from roadmap.md (NOT from FD's archived plan).
Wait for completion.

sub_step.title: Process E2E Generation Subtask Completion
sub_step.action: Extract from completion: E2E framework chosen, run command, test file created, setup notes/warnings.
Verify the test file was actually created at tests/e2e/feature-[sanitized-name].*.

branch.action: Set phase_4_substep="e2e_generated" in state.json. Proceed to Step 5.

Escalate to user via ask_followup_question: "E2E generation subtask completed but did not create the test file. How proceed?" Options: 1. "Retry — re-spawn E2E generation subtask" 2. "Skip E2E for this feature (not recommended)" 3. "I'll write the E2E test manually"

If user selects Retry: re-spawn with more context. Note: the E2E generation prompt instructs the Code subtask to auto-launch a Debug subtask on its own 3rd failure. Allow up to 3 PD-level attempts total (initial spawn + 2 retries). If the 3rd attempt still fails: escalate to user with the Debug subtask's findings (if any) and offer "Skip E2E for this feature" or "I'll write the E2E test manually." Do NOT loop more than 3 times.

If user selects Skip: document in current-state.md as "⚠️ Feature not locked into E2E regression suite — test generation failed." Set phase_4_substep="e2e_generated" (acknowledging skip). Proceed to Step 6 (skip Step 5).
If user selects Manual: wait for user. When they confirm the test file exists, set phase_4_substep="e2e_generated". Proceed to Step 5.

step.title: Validate New E2E Test
step.description: Read the newly generated E2E test file. Verify it correctly implements the roadmap E2E spec. If the test has bugs, spawn Code to fix the E2E TEST (not the app — already verified in Step 2).
step.action: Read the new E2E test file at tests/e2e/feature-[sanitized-name].*.
Compare against the roadmap E2E spec: does the test cover every point? Are the selectors reasonable? Does the test structure follow project conventions?

branch.condition: Test looks correct — covers all E2E spec points, no obvious issues
branch.action: Set phase_4_substep="e2e_validated" in state.json. Proceed to Step 6 (Audit).

branch.condition: Test has bugs or issues — missing spec points, broken selectors, logical errors
branch.action: Spawn a Code subtask to fix the E2E TEST FILE. Include: the test file path, the roadmap E2E spec, and a description of what's wrong. Instruction: "Fix the E2E test. Do NOT modify application code — the app is already verified. Only fix the test file."
Wait for completion. Re-read the test file.
Set phase_4_substep="e2e_validated". Proceed to Step 6.

Escalate to user via ask_followup_question: "E2E test still has issues after fix attempt. How proceed?" Options: 1. "Retry fix" 2. "Accept test as-is (document known issues)" 3. "I'll fix the test manually"
If user selects Retry: re-spawn Code. If still broken: escalate again — do NOT loop more than twice.
If user accepts or fixes manually: set phase_4_substep="e2e_validated". Proceed to Step 6.

step.title: Architectural Alignment Audit
step.action: Check if the agent-workspace/knowledge/ directory exists. If it does: list the directory and read ALL .md files found. These represent what WAS ACTUALLY BUILT. If the directory is empty or missing: note "No knowledge files exist yet" and skip — this is normal for brand-new projects. Do NOT use a project-type file checklist — discover what actually exists on disk.

Read ALL files in the planning/ directory — list the directory and read every .md file found. These represent the ORIGINAL DESIGN INTENT. You will cross-reference them against knowledge/ (as-built reality) to detect architectural drift.

audit_checklist.item: Spaghetti Code: circular dependencies or tightly coupled components?
Duplicated Logic: same patterns described in multiple places without shared abstraction?
Architectural Drift: new feature deviate from the planning/ directory's established architecture?
Wave Integrity: does this feature properly extend Wave N abstractions rather than modifying them?
Tech Debt Signals: TODO markers, "temporary" solutions, bypass patterns?

Knowledge Completeness: all knowledge files in the directory populated with meaningful content? List the directory to discover all files, then check each one. Do NOT flag files as "missing" just because they don't match a project-type template — only flag files that exist but are empty or stale.
Test Coverage Visibility: documentation reference tests/unit/, tests/integration/, and tests/e2e/ coverage?

Oversized Files: Scan the project for files exceeding 500 lines (excluding blank lines and comments). List each with path and line count. For each oversized file, assess: (a) does it contain multiple distinct concerns that should be separate files? (b) could it be a directory instead of a single file? (c) did it grow significantly during this feature (cross-reference with git diff)?

File Growth: Cross-reference the git diff against the previous feature boundary. Flag any file that grew by 200+ lines during this single feature — these are approaching the danger zone even if still under 500 lines.

Long Functions: Search for functions exceeding 50 lines (Code mode's self-refactor limit). List any found — these slipped past the quality loop and indicate Code mode's self-refactor may not be catching them.

Files That Should Be Directories: Identify files whose name suggests a category (e.g., "services", "utils", "helpers", "components") and that contain 3+ unrelated exports. These are directories disguised as files — flag for splitting.

step.action: Assign Architectural Health rating (Excellent/Good/Fair/Poor) with specific evidence.
Set phase_4_substep="audited" in state.json.

step.title: Automatic Lightweight Structural Refactoring
step.description: If the architectural audit found oversized files (>500 lines), spawn a quick Code subtask to split them. This is NOT a full "refactoring feature" — it's a structural cleanup that extracts logical sections into separate files without changing behavior. Runs automatically.
step.trigger: Step 6 audit found one or more files exceeding 500 lines.

skip_conditions.condition: Zero files exceed 500 lines — nothing to refactor. Skip to Step 7.

The same files were already refactored in a previous feature's Phase 4 and have regrown. Detection: read current-state.md "Vision Updates" section and search for "Structural refactoring: split [filename]" entries matching the flagged files. If a match is found: flag for the user instead — "Files [list] have grown back past 500 lines after previous refactoring. This may indicate a structural design issue. Consider a dedicated refactoring feature." Skip to Step 7.

step.title: Spawn Architect to Design the Split
step.action: Spawn an Architect subtask via new_task with: the list of oversized files from the audit (paths + line counts + concern analysis), and instruction: "Design a split plan for these oversized files. For each file, identify logical sections that can become separate files. Specify new file names and directory placement. Follow existing codebase conventions. CRITICAL: When a split produces 3 or more related files that form a logical group, create a new subdirectory for them — do NOT dump multiple new files into the parent directory. The new directory name should reflect the group's purpose (e.g., splitting a 'services.ts' into 'services/auth-provider.ts', 'services/auth-middleware.ts', 'services/auth-types.ts' → create 'services/auth/' with 'provider.ts', 'middleware.ts', 'types.ts'). If a file is a single coherent concern that genuinely cannot be meaningfully split (e.g., a large but unified switch statement, a single-class file with no extractable sections), state this explicitly and explain why — the PD will note it and skip that file. Output a concise plan: old-file → [new-file-1, new-file-2, ...] OR 'Not split — [reason]'. Include any new directories to create. Do NOT implement — only design."
Wait for Architect to complete. Read the Architect's split plan.

filter_not_split.rule: If the Architect marked any files as "Not split — [reason]" (single coherent concern, cannot be meaningfully split): exclude those files from the Code subtask. Note in current-state.md under "Architectural Health": "Files [list] not split — [reason from Architect]." Only proceed with files that have actual split plans.
If ALL files were marked "Not split": skip to Step 7 (Vision Re-evaluation). Nothing to execute.

step.title: Spawn Code to Execute the Split
step.action: Spawn a Code mode subtask via new_task with: the Architect's split plan, and instruction: "Execute this structural refactoring: split the specified oversized files exactly as designed. This is a PURE refactoring — NO behavior changes, NO new features, NO logic modifications. After splitting: run the FULL unfiltered automated test suite. If ANY test fails: revert ALL changes and report the failure. If all tests pass: the work is done. Report: files created, files modified, test results."
Wait for Code to complete.

branch.condition: Code reports test failures or the split could not be completed
branch.action: Do NOT block the feature. Document in current-state.md under "Architectural Health": "⚠️ Oversized files [list] could not be auto-split — [reason from Code subtask]. Manual refactoring needed."
Proceed to Step 7 (Vision Re-evaluation). The feature continues — oversized files are a health warning, not a blocker.

branch.condition: Code reports all tests passed and split was successful
branch.action: Run the FULL existing E2E suite to verify the structural split didn't break anything. The Code subtask ran unit/integration tests, but a structural file split can break E2E tests through import path changes, module resolution shifts, or test file references. If no E2E tests exist (tests/e2e/ empty or missing): skip E2E re-run — nothing to regress. Proceed to reconciliation below.

sub_branch.action: Update vision/ and planning/ files if the split changed file paths referenced in architecture documentation. This is a minor drift — PD edits directly.
Note in current-state.md under "Vision Updates": "Structural refactoring: split [files] into [new-files]. E2E regression: ✅ passed."
Proceed to Step 7 (Vision Re-evaluation).

The refactoring introduced an E2E regression. Spawn a Code subtask to fix the E2E failure — the split is correct, but something in the E2E test or its dependencies broke. Include: the failing E2E output, the split plan, and instruction: "The structural refactoring split [files] into [new-files]. Unit and integration tests pass, but E2E tests now fail. Fix the E2E tests or their dependencies (import paths, selectors, test setup) — do NOT revert the split. Do NOT modify application logic. Run the full E2E suite after fixing."
Wait for Code to complete.

branch.condition: Code fixed the E2E failure and suite passes
branch.action: Note in current-state.md under "Vision Updates": "Structural refactoring: split [files] into [new-files]. E2E regression: fixed (Code subtask)."
Proceed to Step 7 (Vision Re-evaluation).

branch.condition: Code could not fix the E2E failure
branch.action: Do NOT block the feature. Document in current-state.md under "Architectural Health": "⚠️ Structural refactoring of [files] succeeded but E2E suite now fails — [details from Code subtask]. The split is committed; E2E tests need manual attention."
Proceed to Step 7 (Vision Re-evaluation).

step.principle: Lightweight refactoring is preventive maintenance — it keeps files healthy without the overhead of a full refactoring feature. It runs silently (no user prompts) and never blocks the feature pipeline. Failed splits are documented but never halt progress.

step.title: Vision Re-evaluation — Keep the Vision Fluid
step.description: Compare what was just built against the vision documents. Scope drift was already evaluated in Step 3 — this step focuses on vision alignment after audit.
step.action: Read all vision docs in agent-workspace/project-director/vision/.

Read ALL files in the planning/ directory — list the directory and read every .md file found. These represent the design intent. Compare them against the audit findings (Step 6) to detect whether as-built reality diverged from the planned architecture.
Compare the audit findings (Step 6), the drift evaluation (Step 3), and the FD's completion summary against the vision docs. Is the vision still an accurate description of the intended final app?

branch.action: No changes to vision docs. Note in current-state.md under "Vision Updates": "Feature [N] built as envisioned — no vision changes."

branch.condition: MINOR DRIFT — small discrepancies that don't affect other features
branch.action: PD edits the relevant vision .md file(s) and ALL planning/ .md file(s) directly to reflect reality. List the planning/ directory and edit every .md file that needs updating. If any new screens, data entities, or feature-artifact mappings were added, update vision-summary.md's Feature Index table. If the drift involved a technology change, also update the relevant component entry in tech-sourcing.md. If new E2E patterns emerged, update e2e-authoring-guide.md. Note in current-state.md under "Vision Updates": what changed and why.

branch.condition: MAJOR DRIFT — changes that affect remaining roadmap features (not already handled in Step 3)
branch.action: If this drift wasn't already caught in Step 3: follow the same major drift escalation as Step 3 — flag to user, offer re-plan/accept/manual options.
If already handled in Step 3: note that the re-plan is in progress or completed.

step.action: Set phase_4_substep="vision_reevaluated" in state.json.

step.title: Update the Current State Dashboard
step.action: Verify current-state.md exists on disk before reading. If missing: recreate from the dashboard template in common patterns. Announce: "current-state.md was missing — recreated from template."
Read current-state.md and update:

update_sections.section: Append 2-4 bullet points for the new feature's capabilities.
Update UI layout based on FD's report and frontend-ui.md.
Replace with new Health rating and bullet-point findings.
Document any vision changes from Step 7 (what changed, why, re-plan status).
Add/update: total E2E tests, framework used, run command, list of locked-in features, any features not locked in (with reason).

step.action: Set phase_4_substep="dashboarded" in state.json.

Commit Phase 4 changes. Read git_automation_enabled from state.json. If true: Ensure the following Phase 4 artifacts are committed: the new E2E test file in tests/e2e/, the updated current-state.md, and any modified vision docs in agent-workspace/project-director/vision/. Use a commit message summarizing the QA results: feature number, unit/integration test outcome, E2E lock-in status, audit rating, and whether dashboard/vision were updated. If false: announce "Phase 4 changes ready. Please manually commit: tests/e2e/, current-state.md, vision/, and any regression fixes."

step.title: Set Phase State
step.action: Set current_phase=5 and phase_5_substep="phase_set" in state.json.

step.title: Present the Completion Report
step.action: Present a concise report: feature name/number, wave, branch, unit/integration test results, E2E lock-in status (framework + test file), Architectural Health rating with evidence, vision changes, tech debt items.
Set phase_5_substep="report_presented" in state.json.

step.title: The PR/Merge Offer
step.action: Set phase_5_substep="pr_offered" in state.json.
Read git_automation_enabled from state.json.

Use ask_followup_question: "Feature [Name] complete on branch [branch]. Unit tests passed. E2E: [locked ✅ / not locked ⚠️]. Architectural health: [Rating]. Vision: [no changes / minor updates / major re-plan]. How proceed?"
Provide exactly 5 responses: 1. "Push branch and create PR for me (uses GitHub CLI)" 2. "Merge into trunk, push, and clean up — no PR" 3. "I HAVE manually pushed and merged. Proceed to next feature." 4. "I HAVE manually pushed and merged. Inject a refactoring feature." 5. "Hold on, I need to make manual changes."
Provide exactly 3 responses: 1. "I HAVE manually pushed and merged. Proceed to next feature." 2. "I HAVE manually pushed and merged. Inject a refactoring feature." 3. "Hold on, I need to make manual changes." Note: Options 1 (auto-PR) and 2 (auto-merge) are hidden because git automation is disabled.
step.warning: Options 3 and 4 will delete the local feature branch. Ensure your changes have been merged before proceeding.

decision_tree.branch: Proceed to Step 3, Sub-section A (Handle PR Creation).
Proceed to Step 3, Sub-section B (Handle Local Merge).
Set phase_5_substep="pr_handled" in state.json. Proceed to Step 4 (Post-Merge Cleanup).
Set phase_5_substep="pr_handled" in state.json. Proceed to Step 4 (Post-Merge Cleanup). Refactoring injection happens in Step 5.
Wait for user. When ready, re-present the PR/Merge Offer.

step.title: Handle Automated Git Strategy
step.action: Set phase_5_substep="pr_handled" in state.json.

sub_section.name: a_pr_creation
sub_section.trigger: User selects "Push branch and create PR for me"
sub_section.action: Read git_automation_enabled from state.json. If false: skip. Announce "Git automation disabled. Please push and create PR manually." and present post-PR options.

sub_step.action: Push the feature branch to the remote tracking branch. If the push fails, report error to user per error_handling pr_creation_fails scenario.

Create a pull request for the feature branch with title "Feature: [Name]" and a body summarizing test results, architectural audit rating, and vision status. If PR creation tooling is not available, report error per error_handling.
Present PR URL. Use ask_followup_question with 2 responses: 1. "Merged — proceed to next feature" 2. "Merged — inject refactoring feature next"

sub_section.name: b_local_merge
sub_section.trigger: User selects "Merge into trunk, push, and clean up — no PR"
sub_section.action: Read git_automation_enabled from state.json. If false: skip. Announce "Git automation disabled. Please merge and push manually."

sub_step.action: Identify the trunk branch (main, master, develop, or trunk — whichever exists). Checkout the trunk and pull the latest changes.
Merge the feature branch into the trunk. If the merge fails due to conflicts, report error to user per error_handling merge_fails scenario.
Push the trunk branch to remote. If the push fails, report error to user per error_handling merge_fails scenario.
Announce: "Feature '[Name]' merged into [trunk] and pushed. Proceeding to cleanup."

step.action: Proceed to Step 4 (Post-Merge Cleanup).

step.title: Post-Merge Cleanup & Verification
step.trigger: After user confirms merge (or after PR handled in Step 3)

sub_step.title: Update State Before Cleanup (Crash-Safe)
sub_step.action: FIRST: read current_branch_name from state.json and store it in memory as captured_branch_name (needed for substeps 2-3 after we nullify it below).

THEN update state.json BEFORE any branch deletion: increment current_feature_index by 1, set fd_status="idle", phase_4_substep=null, phase_5_substep="state_updated", current_branch_name=null. Keep current_phase=5, status="in_progress". This ensures that if a crash occurs during cleanup, the feature is marked as done and will not be re-executed.

sub_step.title: Verify Branch State
sub_step.action: Check captured_branch_name (stored in substep 0 before state update). If captured_branch_name was null (FD skipped git), skip all destructive cleanup substeps and announce: "No feature branch to clean up. Proceeding." Set phase_5_substep="cleaned_up" and proceed to Step 5.

Read git_automation_enabled from state.json. If false: skip all destructive cleanup, announce "Git automation disabled. Please clean up branches manually." Set phase_5_substep="cleaned_up" and proceed to Step 5.
Identify the trunk branch (main, master, develop, or trunk — whichever exists). Checkout the trunk and pull the latest changes.
Delete the feature branch locally. If the branch doesn't exist (already deleted in a previous partial run): note and continue — this is expected on resume.
Verify the current branch is the trunk branch.

step.action: Announce: "Cleanup complete. On branch [current-branch]. Ready for next feature."
Set phase_5_substep="cleaned_up" in state.json.

step.title: Course Correction & Final State Update
step.action:
If user selected to inject a refactoring feature: edit roadmap.md following these rules:
Insert at position current_feature_index (the feature just completed) + 1. Increment subsequent feature numbers. Name descriptively based on audit findings (e.g., "Feature [N]: Refactoring — Extract Shared Auth Hook"). Place in same wave unless refactoring spans entire codebase. Cite specific evidence from architectural audit. Increment total_features in state.json by 1.
If scope-creep ideas were mentioned: add to backlog.md with date and proposed-by.

Set current_phase=3, phase_5_substep=null in state.json. (current_feature_index was already incremented in Step 4 substep 0; fd_status="idle", phase_4_substep=null, current_branch_name=null also already set.) Keep status="in_progress".
Loop back to Phase 3 Step 1 for the next feature.

completion_criteria.criterion: All features in the roadmap built and merged (current_feature_index == total_features).
All unit and integration tests pass for the complete project.
All E2E regression tests pass. Any feature not locked in documented in current-state.md with reason.
current-state.md dashboard reflects all completed features, UI map, vision updates, and E2E regression suite status.
Vision docs accurately reflect the true built state of the app (all minor drifts reconciled).
Architectural health assessment is "Good" or better, or all tech debt items documented in backlog.md.
All feature branches cleaned up locally.
